---
title: "Parenthood, Immigration and Further Education"
subtitle: "Analyses and Visualizations"
author: "Nate Breznau, Johannes Bonnes and Sylvia Rahn"
output:
  word_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: '1'
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    number_sections: true
    toc_depth: 1
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: '1'
---


## Setup   

```{r setup, echo = F, warning = F, message = F}

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

packages <- c('tidyverse',
              'readr',
              'knitr',
              'here',
              'ggplot2',
              'ggrepel',
              'ragg',
              'kableExtra',
              'countrycode',
              'readxl',
              'jtools',
              'lme4',
              'lmerTest',
              'broom.mixed',
              'viridis')

pacman::p_load(packages, character.only = T)

```

## Data

```{r }


#df <- read_rds(here("Data", "df.RDS"))
df2 <- read_rds(here("Data", "df2.RDS"))

# basic or lower literacy

df2 <- df2 %>%
  mutate(reading_low = ifelse(literacy_level %in% c(0, 1), 1, 0))

#df <- df %>%
#  mutate(reading_low = ifelse(literacy_level %in% c(0, 1), 1, 0))

```

## Descriptives

### Immigration, Low Literacy, Sex, Parenthood

The number of immigrants with basic needs

```{r}

basic_needs <- df2 %>%
  mutate(group_imm = case_when(female == 1 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 0 ~ 1,
                               female == 1 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 1 ~ 2,
                               female == 1 & FORBORN == 0 & children == 0 ~ 3,
                               female == 1 & FORBORN == 0 & children == 1 ~ 4,
                               female == 0 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 0 ~ 5,
                               female == 0 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 1 ~ 6,
                               female == 0 & FORBORN == 0 & children == 0 ~ 7,
                               female == 0 & FORBORN == 0 & children == 1 ~ 8,
                               .default = NA_real_)
         ) %>%
  group_by(group_imm) %>%
  summarise(
    weighted_low = sum(SPFWT0 * reading_low, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_weight = sum(SPFWT0, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  mutate(percent_low = 100 * weighted_low / total_weight)

basic_needs <- basic_needs %>%
  mutate(label = case_match(group_imm,
                            1 ~ "Foreign-Born Woman, No Children",
                            2 ~ "Foreign-Born Woman, Children",
                            3 ~ "Native-Born Woman, No Children",
                            4 ~ "Native-Born Woman, Children",
                            5 ~ "Foreign-Born Man, No Children",
                            6 ~ "Foreign-Born Man, Children",
                            7 ~ "Native-Born Man, No Children",
                            8 ~ "Native-Born Man, Children"))

```


## Country-Level Associations

### Coding

```{r g07}
  
group_07 <- df2 %>%
  filter(!is.na(group_07)) %>%
  group_by(iso3c, group_07) %>%
  summarise(
    weighted_count = sum(SPFWT0, na.rm = TRUE),
    weighted_skill_reading = sum(skill_reading * SPFWT0, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(skill_reading)]),
    weighted_skill_numeracy = sum(skill_numeracy * SPFWT0, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(skill_numeracy)]),
    fet = sum(NFE12 * SPFWT0, na.rm = T) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(NFE12)])
  ) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  mutate(percent = (weighted_count / sum(weighted_count)) * 100) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(year = 2022)

group_07 <- group_07 %>%
  mutate(label = case_when(group_07 == 4 ~ "Native-born, Children",
                           group_07 == 3 ~ "Native-born, no Children",
                           group_07 == 2 ~ "Foreign-Born, Children",
                           group_07 == 1 ~ "Foreign-Born, no Children",
                           .default = NA))

# Filter immigrant mothers
immigrant_mothers <- group_07 %>%
  filter(group_07 == 2) %>%
  select(iso3c, percent, weighted_skill_reading) %>%
  rename(
    percent_immigrant = percent,
    reading_immigrant = weighted_skill_reading
  )

# Get native mothers' reading scores
native_mothers <- group_07 %>%
  filter(group_07 == 4) %>%
  select(iso3c, weighted_skill_reading) %>%
  rename(reading_native = weighted_skill_reading)

immigrant_mothers_fet <- group_07 %>%
  filter(group_07 == 2) %>%
  select(iso3c, fet) %>%
  rename(immigrant_mothers_fet = fet)

native_mothers_fet <- group_07 %>%
  filter(group_07 == 4) %>%
  select(iso3c, fet) %>%
  rename(native_mothers_fet = fet)

# Join and compute skill gap
plot_data <- immigrant_mothers %>%
  left_join(native_mothers, by = "iso3c") %>%
  left_join(immigrant_mothers_fet, by = "iso3c") %>%
  left_join(native_mothers_fet, by = "iso3c") %>%
  mutate(skill_gap = reading_native - reading_immigrant,
         fet_gap = native_mothers_fet - immigrant_mothers_fet)

# Compute Pearson's correlation and store results
cor_test <- cor.test(plot_data$immigrant_mothers_fet, plot_data$reading_immigrant)
cor_test2 <- cor.test(plot_data$fet_gap, plot_data$skill_gap)

# Optionally round them to 3 digits in variables for later reuse
cor_coef <- round(cor_test$estimate, 3)
cor_pval <- round(cor_test$p.value, 3)
cor_coef2 <- round(cor_test2$estimate, 3)
cor_pval2 <- round(cor_test2$p.value, 3)


```


### Figure 1. FET and Skills

Here we see a strong association of participation in further education courses in the last 12 months (x-axis) and the reading skill level for immigrant mothers (y-axis) at the country-level (r = `r cor_coef`, p = `r cor_pval`). It also appears that in countries with a higher percentage of immigrant mothers (darker blue point), the overall average reading skills are lower. There is little or no association between the reading skill gap between natives and immigrants (size of the point) and the x- or y-axis.


```{r}


ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = immigrant_mothers_fet, y = reading_immigrant)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = abs(skill_gap), color = percent_immigrant), alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = iso3c), size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf) +
  guides(
    size = guide_legend(title = "Skill gap\nto native\nmothers"),
    color = guide_colorbar(title = "Immigrant\nmothers\n% of pop."),
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(
    name = "Percent immigrant mothers",
    low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Immigrant mother participation in further education,\nlast 12 months rate",
    y = "Reading skills of immigrant mothers,\npopulation weighted average",
    title = "Further Education and Reading Skills of Immigrant Mothers"
    #, subtitle = "Point size shows skill gap with native mothers"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() 




```

### Figure 2. FET and Skills Gap


There is a strong association of having a larger gap in further education participation between natives and immigrant mothers (x-axis) and the readings skills gap between natives and immigrant mothers (y-axis) (r = `r cor_coef2`, p = `r cor_pval2`).



```{r}

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = fet_gap, y = skill_gap)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = percent_immigrant), size = 4) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = iso3c), size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf) +
  guides(
    size = "none",
    color = guide_colorbar(title = "Immigrant\nmothers\n% of pop.")
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient(
    name = "Percent immigrant mothers",
    low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Further education participation of mothers,\nnative-immigrant average gap",
    y = "Reading skills of mothers,\nnative-immigrant average gap",
    title = "Further Education and Reading Skill Gaps\nof Mothers by Immigration Background"
    #, subtitle = "Point size shows skill gap with native mothers"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

## Descriptives

```{r}

table_1 <- df2 %>%
  subset(female == 1) %>%
    mutate(
    ed_primary   = ifelse(ed3 == 1, 1, 0),
    ed_secondary = ifelse(ed3 == 2, 1, 0),
    ed_tertiary  = ifelse(ed3 == 3, 1, 0),
    missing = case_when(is.na(skill_reading) | is.na(NFE12) | is.na(FORBORN) | is.na(children) | is.na(FORLANG) | is.na(ed3) | is.na(ed_currently) ~ 1,
                        .default = NA)
    ) %>%
  select(-ed3) %>%
  select(iso3c, skill_reading, NFE12, FORBORN, children, FORLANG, ed_primary, ed_secondary, ed_tertiary, ed_currently, missing)

  
# drop countries that do not have all variables
valid_countries <- table_1 %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(!is.na(.)))) %>%
  filter(across(-iso3c, ~ . > 0)) %>%
  pull(iso3c)

table_1 <- table_1 %>%
  filter(iso3c %in% valid_countries)

table_1out <- table_1 %>%
  select(-iso3c) %>%
  summarise(across(
    everything(),
    list(
      mean = ~ mean(., na.rm = TRUE),
      sd   = ~ sd(., na.rm = TRUE),
      min  = ~ min(., na.rm = TRUE),
      max  = ~ max(., na.rm = TRUE),
      n    = ~ sum(!is.na(.))
    ),
    .names = "{.col}__{.fn}"  # double underscore so as not to confuse the var names
  )) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to = c("variable", ".value"),
    names_sep = "__"  
  )

table_1out %>%
  mutate(variable = case_when(
    variable == "skill_reading"   ~ "Literacy Skills",
    variable == "NFE12"           ~ "ALE Participation",
    variable == "FORBORN"         ~ "Foreign-Born",
    variable == "children"        ~ "Has Children",
    variable == "FORLANG"         ~ "Non-Native Speaker",
    variable == "ed_currently"    ~ "Currently in Education",
    variable == "ed_primary"      ~ "Primary Education",
    variable == "ed_secondary"    ~ "Secondary Education",
    variable == "ed_tertiary"     ~ "Tertiary Education",
    variable == "missing"         ~ "Listwise Missing Cases",
    TRUE ~ variable
  )) %>%
  kable("html", digits = 2, caption = "Descriptive Statistics") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  save_kable(here("Results", "table_S1.html"))

table_1out  %>% kable("html", digits = 2, caption = "Descriptive Statistics") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"))


```


## Regressions

### Basic

We do not use the weighting variables here because we are focusing in on specific groups, and the weights are for the full population.



```{r}
df_fem_imm <- subset(df2, FORBORN == 1 & female == 1 & !is.na(NFE12) & !is.na(children) & !is.na(FORLANG) & !is.na(ed3) & !is.na(ed_currently))

df_fem_nat <- subset(df2, FORBORN == 0 & female == 1 & !is.na(NFE12) & !is.na(children) & !is.na(FORLANG) & !is.na(ed3) & !is.na(ed_currently))

m1_imm_1 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + children + FORLANG + factor(ed3) + factor(iso3c), data = df_fem_imm)

m1_nat_1 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + children + FORLANG + factor(ed3) + factor(iso3c), data = df_fem_nat)

m1_imm_2 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + children + FORLANG + factor(ed3) + ed_currently + factor(iso3c), data = df_fem_imm)

m1_nat_2 <- lm(skill_reading ~ NFE12 + children + FORLANG + factor(ed3) + ed_currently + factor(iso3c), data = df_fem_nat)

export_summs(
  m1_imm_1, m1_nat_1, m1_imm_2, m1_nat_2,
  model.names = c("Foreign-born 1", "Native-born 1", "Foreign-born 2", "Native-born 2"),
  coefs = c(
    "(Intercept)",
    "NFE12",
    "children",
    "FORLANG",
    "factor(ed3)2",
    "factor(ed3)3",
    "ed_currently"
  ),
  to.file = "html",
  file.name = here("Results", "Reg_Table_1.html")
)

include_graphics(here("Results", "Reg_Table_1.html"))

```
### Test for Sample Bias

Here we correlate the residuals with the weighting variable. If this is close to zero then we can look at our sample as a representative treatment for ALE and literacy. 

```{r}

df_fem_imm$resid_imm_1 <- residuals(m1_imm_1)
df_fem_nat$resid_nat_1 <- residuals(m1_nat_1)
df_fem_imm$resid_imm_2 <- residuals(m1_imm_2)
df_fem_nat$resid_nat_2 <- residuals(m1_nat_2)

testcor1 <- cor(df_fem_imm$aweight, df_fem_imm$resid_imm_1)
testcor2 <- cor(df_fem_imm$aweight, df_fem_imm$resid_imm_2)
testcor3 <- cor(df_fem_nat$aweight, df_fem_nat$resid_nat_1)
testcor4 <- cor(df_fem_nat$aweight, df_fem_nat$resid_nat_2)

```

Correlations are `r round(testcor1,2`, `r round(testcor2,2`, `r round(testcor3,2`, & `r round(testcor4,2`. This suggests that there is no need to weight the sample.

### Multilevel

#### Recodes

```{r}


df2 <- df2 %>%
  mutate(
    ed3_secondary = ifelse(ed3 == 2, 1, 0),
    ed3_tertiary = ifelse(ed3 == 3, 1, 0)
  )

# within/between decomposition
df2 <- df2 %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  mutate(
    # weighted means are necessary at the country level
    NFE12_between = sum(SPFWT0 * NFE12, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(NFE12)], na.rm = TRUE),
    children_between = sum(SPFWT0 * children, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(children)], na.rm = TRUE),
    ed_currently_between = sum(SPFWT0 * ed_currently, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(ed_currently)], na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_secondary_between = sum(SPFWT0 * ed3_secondary, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(ed3_secondary)], na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_tertiary_between = sum(SPFWT0 * ed3_tertiary, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(ed3_tertiary)], na.rm = TRUE),
    forborn_between = sum(SPFWT0 * FORLANG, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(FORLANG)], na.rm = TRUE),
    forlang_between = sum(SPFWT0 * FORBORN, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(SPFWT0[!is.na(FORBORN)], na.rm = TRUE),
    NFE12_within = NFE12 - NFE12_between,
    children_within = children - children_between,
    ed_currently_within = ed_currently - ed_currently_between,
    ed3_secondary_within = ed3_secondary - ed3_secondary_between,
    ed3_tertiary_within = ed3_tertiary - ed3_tertiary_between,
    forborn_within = FORBORN - forborn_between,
    forlang_within = FORLANG - forlang_between
  ) %>%
  ungroup()


# the within effects need to be further centered by group
df2_imm <- df2 %>%
  subset(FORBORN == 1 & female == 1) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  mutate(
    NFE12_within = NFE12_within - mean(NFE12_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    children_within = children_within - mean(children_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    ed_currently_within = ed_currently_within - mean(ed_currently_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_secondary_within = ed3_secondary_within - mean(ed3_secondary_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_tertiary_within = ed3_tertiary_within - mean(ed3_tertiary_within, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

df2_nat <- df2 %>%
  subset(FORBORN == 0 & female == 1) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  mutate(
    NFE12_within = NFE12_within - mean(NFE12_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    children_within = children_within - mean(children_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    ed_currently_within = ed_currently_within - mean(ed_currently_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_secondary_within = ed3_secondary_within - mean(ed3_secondary_within, na.rm = TRUE),
    ed3_tertiary_within = ed3_tertiary_within - mean(ed3_tertiary_within, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

```


#### Models

We first look at immigrants and native-born separately. Then we combine them to determine if there is an 'immigrant penalty' after controlling for participation in further education and other variables. 

```{r}

m1_imm_mlm <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between +
    children_within + children_between +
    forlang_within + forlang_between +
    #ed_currently_within + ed_currently_between +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between +
    gdppc +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = df2_imm
)

ranef_list <- ranef(m1_imm_mlm)

# Get country-level grand intercept deviations
iso3c_intercepts <- ranef_list$iso3c



m1_nat_mlm <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between +
    children_within + children_between +
    forlang_within + forlang_between +
    #ed_currently_within + ed_currently_between +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between +
    gdppc +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = df2_nat
)

m2 <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between +
    children_within + children_between +
    forlang_within + forlang_between +
    forborn_within + forborn_between +
    #ed_currently_within + ed_currently_between +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between +
    gdppc +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = subset(df2, female == 1)
)

m3 <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between +
    children_within + children_between +
    forlang_within + forlang_between +
    forborn_within + forborn_between +
    forborn_within*NFE12_within +
    #ed_currently_within + ed_currently_between +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between +
    gdppc +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = subset(df2, female == 1)
)

```

#### Results


```{r}

export_summs(
  m1_imm_mlm, m1_nat_mlm, m2, m3,
  model.names = c("Foreign-born", "Native-born", "Pooled", "Pooled with ALE interaction"),
  to.file = "html",
  file.name = here("Results", "Reg_Table_2.html"
))

```



### ATEs

Type 1: Foreign-born mother, non-native speaker
c(forborn_within = 1, children_within = 1, forlang_within = 1)

Type 2: Foreign-born woman without children, non-native speaker" = c(forborn_within = 1, children_within = 0, forlang_within = 1)

Type 3: Foreign-born mother, native speaker" = c(forborn_within = 1, children_within = 1, forlang_within = 0)

Type 4: Foreign-born woman without children, native speaker" = c(forborn_within = 1, children_within = 0, forlang_within = 0)

Type 5: Native-born mother, native speaker" = c(forborn_within = 0, children_within = 1, forlang_within = 0)

Type 6: Native-born woman without children, native speaker" = c(forborn_within = 0, children_within = 0, forlang_within = 0)
)

#### T S2. Interactive GLM

Here we interact all intersectional variables of interest with ALE (NFE12) to see the unique effects if any.

```{r}
m4 <- lmer(
  skill_reading ~ 
    NFE12_within + NFE12_between +
    children_within + children_between +
    forlang_within + forlang_between +
    forborn_within + forborn_between +
    # interaction with all sub-interactions
    forborn_within*children_within*forlang_within*NFE12_within +
    #ed_currently_within + ed_currently_between +
    ed3_secondary_within + ed3_secondary_between +
    ed3_tertiary_within + ed3_tertiary_between +
    gdppc +
    (1 | iso3c),
  data = subset(df2, female == 1)
)

export_summs(
  m4,
  coefs = c(
    "(Intercept)" = "(Intercept)",
    "ALE (within)" = "NFE12_within",
    "ALE (between)" = "NFE12_between",
    "Children (within)" = "children_within",
    "Children (between)" = "children_between",
    "Non-native speaker (within)" = "forlang_within",
    "Non-native speaker (between)" = "forlang_between",
    "Foreign-born (within)" = "forborn_within",
    "Foreign-born (between)" = "forborn_between",
    "Secondary education (within)" = "ed3_secondary_within",
    "Secondary education (between)" = "ed3_secondary_between",
    "Tertiary education (within)" = "ed3_tertiary_within",
    "Tertiary education (between)" = "ed3_tertiary_between",
    "GDP per capita" = "gdppc",
    "Children x Foreign-born (within)" = "children_within:forborn_within",
    "Non-native x Foreign-born (within)" = "forlang_within:forborn_within",
    "Children x Non-native (within)" = "children_within:forlang_within",
    "ALE x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Children (within)" = "NFE12_within:children_within",
    "ALE x Non-native (within)" = "NFE12_within:forlang_within",
    "Children x Non-native x Foreign-born (within)" = "children_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Children x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:children_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Non-native x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:forlang_within:forborn_within",
    "ALE x Children x Non-native x Foreign-born (within)" = "NFE12_within:children_within:forlang_within:forborn_within"
  ),
  to.file = "html",
  file.name = here("Results", "Reg_Table_S2.html")
)

include_graphics(here("Results", "Reg_Table_S2.html"))
```
#### Predictions

```{r}

df2r <- subset(df2, !is.na(NFE12) & !is.na(children_within) & !is.na(forborn_within) & !is.na(forlang_within) & !is.na(ed3) & !is.na(gdppc) & !is.na(skill_reading))

#Type 1: Foreign-born mother, non-native speaker
df_type10 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type11 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 1)

#Type 2: Foreign-born woman without children, non-native
df_type20 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type21 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 1,
         NFE12_within = 1)

#Type 3: Foreign-born mother, native speaker
df_type30 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type31 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

#Type 4: Foreign-born woman without children, native speaker
df_type40 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type41 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 1,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

#Type 5: Native-born mother, native speaker
df_type50 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type51 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 1,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

#Type 6: Native-born woman without children, native speaker
df_type60 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 0)

df_type61 <- subset(df2r, female == 1) %>%
  mutate(forborn_within = 0,
         children_within = 0,
         forlang_within = 0,
         NFE12_within = 1)

# make predictions
pred10 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type10, re.form = NA)
pred11 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type11, re.form = NA)
pred20 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type20, re.form = NA)
pred21 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type21, re.form = NA)
pred30 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type30, re.form = NA)
pred31 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type31, re.form = NA)
pred40 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type40, re.form = NA)
pred41 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type41, re.form = NA)
pred50 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type50, re.form = NA)
pred51 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type51, re.form = NA)
pred60 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type60, re.form = NA)
pred61 <- predict(m4, newdata = df_type61, re.form = NA)

# Calculate average marginal effect
diff1 <- pred10 - pred11
diff2 <- pred20 - pred21
diff3 <- pred30 - pred31
diff4 <- pred40 - pred41
diff5 <- pred50 - pred51
diff6 <- pred60 - pred61

AME1 <- mean(diff1)
ttest1 <- t.test(pred11, pred10, paired = FALSE)
AME2 <- mean(diff2)
ttest2 <- t.test(pred21, pred20, paired = FALSE)
AME3 <- mean(diff3)
ttest3 <- t.test(pred31, pred30, paired = FALSE)
AME4 <- mean(diff4)
ttest4 <- t.test(pred41, pred40, paired = FALSE)
AME5 <- mean(diff5)
ttest5 <- t.test(pred51, pred50, paired = FALSE)
AME6 <- mean(diff6)
ttest6 <- t.test(pred61, pred60, paired = FALSE)



```


#### Plot

##### Combine values

```{r}



ame_df <- data.frame(
  type = c(
    "1. Foreign-born mother\nnon-native speaker",
    "2. Foreign-born no children\nnon-native speaker",
    "3. Foreign-born mother\nnative speaker",
    "4. Foreign-born no children\nnative speaker",
    "5. Native-born mother\nnative speaker",
    "6. Native-born no children\nnative speaker"
  ),
  AME = c(AME1, AME2, AME3, AME4, AME5, AME6),
  lower = c(ttest1$conf.int[1], ttest2$conf.int[1], ttest3$conf.int[1],
            ttest4$conf.int[1], ttest5$conf.int[1], ttest6$conf.int[1]),
  upper = c(ttest1$conf.int[2], ttest2$conf.int[2], ttest3$conf.int[2],
            ttest4$conf.int[2], ttest5$conf.int[2], ttest6$conf.int[2])
)

ame_df <- ame_df %>%
  mutate(
    AME = -AME,
    lower = lower,
    upper = upper
  )




```

##### Plot

```{r}

agg_png(here("Results", "Fig1.png"), res = 144, height = 600, width = 750)

ggplot(ame_df, aes(x = type, y = AME, fill = type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "D", begin = 0.1, end = 0.9) +
  labs(
    title = NULL,
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average Marginal Effect\nof Participation in ALE",
    fill = "Type"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

dev.off()

include_graphics(here("Results", "Fig1.png"))


```

### Population Predictions

Here we calculate how much skills would improve if every non-participant in ALE got a skill boost equal to the average in group 1 v. group 6. 

#### Predict

```{r}



# Set your AMEs
AME1 <- -mean(pred10 - pred11)  # gain from ALE for Type 1
AME6 <- -mean(pred60 - pred61)  # gain from ALE for Type 6

# Subset to female respondents
df_female <- df2 %>% filter(female == 1)

# STEP 1: boost literacy for Type 1 non-participants
df_female <- df_female %>%
  mutate(
    boost_type1 = ifelse(
      NFE12 == 0 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 1 & FORLANG == 1,
      skill_reading + AME1,
      skill_reading
    ),
    boost_type6 = ifelse(
      NFE12 == 0 & FORBORN == 0 & children == 0 & FORLANG == 0,
      skill_reading + AME6,
      skill_reading
    )
  )

# STEP 2: weighted mean literacy per country
summary_by_country <- subset(df_female, !is.na(children) & !is.na(FORBORN) & !is.na(FORLANG)) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarise(
    skill_base = weighted.mean(skill_reading, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    skill_type1boost = weighted.mean(boost_type1, aweight, na.rm = TRUE) - skill_base,
    skill_type6boost = weighted.mean(boost_type6, aweight, na.rm = TRUE) - skill_base
  )

# STEP 3: calculate percent of total population who are Type 1
type1_shares <- df2 %>%
  mutate(type1 = ifelse(
    female == 1 & FORBORN == 1 & children == 1 & FORLANG == 1,
    1, 0),
    type6_noALE = ifelse(FORBORN == 0 & NFE12 == 0 & children == 0 & FORLANG == 0, 1, 0 )) %>%
  group_by(iso3c) %>%
  summarise(
    pct_type1_totalpop = weighted.mean(type1, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_type6_noALE_totalpop = weighted.mean(type6_noALE, aweight, na.rm = TRUE),
  )

# STEP 4: merge results
final_df <- left_join(summary_by_country, type1_shares, by = "iso3c")

# Optional: print or inspect
print(final_df)

```

#### Plot

```{r}


agg_png(here("Results", "Fig2.png"), res = 144, height = 750, width = 1000)

ggplot(final_df, aes(
  x = skill_type1boost,
  y = skill_type6boost,
  label = iso3c,
  size = pct_type1_totalpop
)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey40") +
  geom_point(aes(color = pct_type6_noALE_totalpop), alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_text_repel(size = 3, max.overlaps = Inf) +
scale_size_continuous(
  range = c(1, 6),
  name = "% Type 1\nof tot pop",
  breaks = c(0.001, 0.01, 0.03, 0.05),  
  labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
) +
  scale_color_gradient(
  name = "% Type 6\nno ALE",
  low = "lightblue", high = "darkblue",
  labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)
) +
  labs(
    title = "Simulated National Literacy Gains from Targeted ALE",
    subtitle = "Boost in average female literacy if all non-participants in each group joined ALE",
    x = "Overall gain to average literacy if all type 1 without ALE\nwere given an ALE treatment effect",
    y = "Overall gain to average literacy if all type 6 without ALE\nwere given an ALE treatment effect"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10)
  )

dev.off()

include_graphics(here("Results", "Fig2.png"))



```
